objective: Unify K-12 financial, enrollment, and achievement data from multiple US education sources into comprehensive state-level datasets spanning 1986-2019
sources: Urban Institute, National Center for Education Statistics (NCES), US Census Bureau
features_description: Comprehensive state-level indicators including financial metrics (revenue, expenditure by source and category), enrollment statistics (by grade, race, gender), academic achievement scores (NAEP math and reading), demographic breakdowns, and temporal trends across 33 years
representativity_description: Complete coverage of all 50 US states plus DC across 33 years (1986-2019), providing the most comprehensive longitudinal view of American K-12 education system

missing_values.description: Some indicators missing depending on states and years due to data collection variations across different survey years

demographics: Race and gender breakdowns by grade (AM, AS, HI, BL, WH, HP, TR)

states_all.csv.description: Primary data file with aggregated state-level data
states_all.csv.focus: Core financial, enrollment, and achievement indicators

states_all_extended.csv.description: Extended version with detailed race and gender breakdowns
states_all_extended.csv.focus: Granular demographic analysis with all possible combinations

column_details.description: Combination of year and state name (e.g., '1992_ALABAMA')
US Census Bureau count of students in the state
Total revenue for the state education system
Total expenditure for the state education system
State average score for 4th graders taking NAEP math exam
State average score for 4th graders taking NAEP reading exam

usage_notes: This is the most comprehensive longitudinal dataset of US K-12 education available. Perfect for policy analysis, state comparisons, trend identification, and predictive modeling. The extended version with 6697 columns provides unparalleled granularity for demographic analysis.
potential_applications: Longitudinal trends in education funding and outcomes
Predictive modeling of student achievement based on financial inputs
Demographic achievement gap analysis across race and gender
Time series forecasting of education metrics
Federal vs state vs local funding impact studies
Correlation analysis between spending categories and outcomes
Geographic clustering of education performance patterns
Policy effectiveness evaluation across different states
research_value: This dataset enables unprecedented research into American education policy effectiveness, funding mechanisms, demographic equity, and longitudinal trends. The 33-year timespan covers major education reforms and policy changes, making it invaluable for causal inference studies.
processing_notes: Due to the massive size (6697 columns), this dataset requires careful memory management and chunked processing. The extended file may exceed typical memory limits and require distributed processing approaches.
